Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to parse the normative process description and event log The normative process steps in order Receive order Check inventory must occur before payment authorization decisions Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Also if inventory insufficient after Check inventory go to Cancel order directly then Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus the correct sequences are Case with sufficient inventory Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Additionally any order where inventory insufficient Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order Thus any deviation for example Authorize payment before Check inventory Packaging before Authorize Shipping before Packaging would be a violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive order OK Check inventory OK Authorize payment OK Package items OK Ship order OK Close order OK All in correct order No violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive order OK Check inventory OK Package items This is after Check inventory but before Authorize payment Violation because Authorize payment should come before Package items Authorize payment After Package items wrong order Ship order after Authorize payment but Package before Authorize so still violation Close order after Ship order acceptable but prior steps are wrong Thus Case 2 violates normative process

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Case 3 Receive order OK Check inventory OK Authorize payment OK after Check inventory Cancel order after Authorize payment only if inventory insufficient The log does not record the inventory result but going to Cancel order implies insufficient inventory

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For the insufficient inventory path the process should go from Check inventory directly to Cancel order and then Close order Here the case includes Authorize payment before Cancel order and then even Ship order after Cancel order Both conflict with the normative description Thus Case 3 violates

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive order OK Check inventory OK Cancel order after Check inventory implying insufficient inventory then Close order This matches the shorter cancellation sequence Receive Check Cancel Close No violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive order OK Check inventory OK Authorize payment OK Ship order occurs without Package items Therefore this violates the rule that Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping
